content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation for Bias Mitigation in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for LLMs often fail to prevent the generation of biased or stereotypical content, especially when implicit cues are present. There is a need for prompt engineering strategies that robustly reduce social bias in model outputs.",
  "motivation": "Social biases in language models can perpetuate harmful stereotypes and reinforce societal inequities. As LLMs are increasingly deployed in sensitive applications, mitigating such biases at the prompt level is critical for responsible AI. Advances in counterfactual data generation present a timely opportunity to improve bias mitigation via prompt design.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation (CPA), a method that dynamically rewrites user prompts by generating counterfactual variants—altering demographics (e.g., gender, race, age) while preserving semantic meaning. The model is prompted with both the original and counterfactual prompts in parallel, and a consistency regularization loss is applied to align their outputs. During inference, CPA can be integrated as a prompt preprocessor or as part of a pipeline that filters or ensembles outputs to mitigate bias.",
  "experiment_plan": "Experiments will use the StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs datasets to evaluate social bias in generations. Baselines include standard prompting, rephrasing, and debiasing post-processing methods. Metrics will include bias scores (e.g., StereoSet intrasentence scores), output consistency, and human evaluation for stereotype presence. Ablations will assess the impact of counterfactual diversity and regularization strength."
}
